kill_fact: No kill_fact found. The load-bearing numbers all verify against primary sources: Q3 FY26 revenue $1.214B (+39% YoY) and non-GAAP EPS $3.72 (beat ~$3.54) confirmed in the SEC 8-K/Ex-99.1 and the May 4 2026 call; DCI $197M (+90% YoY); customer concentration NVIDIA 27.6% + Cisco 18.2% = 45.8% confirmed verbatim in the FY2025 10-K (filed 08/19/2025). The sop_fair_value ($520) is NOT a stale anchor — it is built on the most recent quarter (Mar-2026) plus the Q4 guide. The CPO/Spectrum-X Photonics displacement risk is real and dated (Ethernet CPO switches shipping 2026), but NVIDIA's own Spectrum-X Photonics announcement explicitly names Fabrinet as a pluggable-transceiver partner ON THE SAME PLATFORM, so it is a multi-year mix-shift, not a cliff — consistent with the debate's 'manageable, not terminal' framing. The thesis survives; the B verdict (own only on a basket-wide AI de-grossing flush toward ~$520/$385, not at ~$574 with optionality already capitalized) is the correct, already-conservative stance.
corrections: (1) Entry-context nuance: the dossier frames the price as 'now ~$574' against a post-print aftermarket of ~$713 — i.e. the stock has ALREADY pulled back ~19% from its post-Q3 high. This modestly improves the entry vs the dossier's narrative but it is still ~9% ABOVE the $520 base fair value, so the unfavorable risk/reward (~0.6:1) and B verdict stand. (2) Forward multiple verifies at ~35x (stockanalysis.com), at the HIGH end of the debate's '32-35x forward' band — the multiple, not the numbers, remains the documented vulnerability. (3) 52-wk low cited as $262 in the debate; primary sources show $258-$262 (immaterial).
evidence: SEC Form 8-K / Ex-99.1, Fabrinet Q3 FY2026 results (quarter ended Mar 27 2026), filed May 4 2026: record revenue $1.214B (+39% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $3.72; Q4 FY26 guide $1.25-1.29B / EPS $3.72-3.87 — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001408710/000140871026000014/

Fabrinet FY2025 Form 10-K (filed 08/19/2025), customer-concentration disclosure: NVIDIA 27.6% and Cisco 18.2% of FY2025 revenue (= 45.8% two-customer), down from 48.5% in FY2024 — investor.fabrinet.com / sec.gov EDGAR CIK 0001408710 fn-20250627

NVIDIA Newsroom, 'NVIDIA Announces Spectrum-X Photonics, Co-Packaged Optics Networking Switches' (GTC 2025): Quantum-X Photonics InfiniBand later in 2025, Spectrum-X Photonics Ethernet in 2026; lists Coherent, Eoptolink, Fabrinet, Innolight as pluggable-transceiver partners on the platform — nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-spectrum-x-co-packaged-optics-networking-switches-ai-factories

stockanalysis.com/stocks/fn (read June 22 2026): price ~$573.76 (close Jun 18 2026), market cap ~$20.6B, forward P/E ~35.0, 52-wk range $258-$748.89 — confirms current price ~9% above the $520 base SoP fair value
